answers: Screenplay format: scene headings, action lines, character names, dialogue. Use proper formatting software (WriterSolo, Final Draft).
Structure: three acts — setup (25%), confrontation (50%), resolution (25%). Every scene should move the story forward.
Tips: show don't tell, write visually, keep dialogue natural and subtext-rich, and rewrite multiple times.
Screenwriting golden rule: enter scenes late, leave early. Cut everything that doesn't serve the story.
